scope.summary: Rust block_on reporting now consumes nominal tokio runtime evidence for proven self-field Runtime and Handle receivers.

capability_alignment.capability: receiver type provenance for field-backed future drive scheduling attribution
not_a_feature_list: No exact admission path was opened for Rust block_on or async/sync equivalence.
No new public semantic-pack API was added.
The verifier reuses node-anchored DomainEvidence::Nominal instead of adding a block_on-specific field feature.
Only exact self.field receiver nodes receive field-domain proof; selector-only .block_on and arbitrary field reads stay closed.
Local structs, type aliases, wrapper fields, constructor-assigned fields, project-local tokio roots or aliases, and non-self receivers remain closed.
reused_capabilities: shared future-drive scheduling and future-settled value-channel obligations

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: use tokio::runtime::Runtime; struct Runner { rt: Runtime } impl Runner { fn run(&self) { self.rt.block_on(...) } }
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The receiver must be self.field inside an impl method with a self parameter, and the same module scope must contain exactly one matching struct field whose type is backed by exact tokio::runtime::Runtime import evidence.

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: struct Runner { handle: tokio::runtime::Handle } impl Runner { fn run(&self) { self.handle.block_on(...) } }
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: Fully qualified tokio runtime field types are accepted only when the file does not define a project-local tokio root.

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: use tokio::{runtime::{Runtime}}; struct Runner { rt: Runtime } impl Runner { fn run(&self) { self.rt.block_on(...) } }
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: Nested static brace import evidence composes with the same field-domain proof.

assertions: Imported tokio Runtime self-field domain evidence depends on the imported binding symbol evidence.
Fully qualified tokio Handle self-field domain evidence is accepted without import dependencies.
Non-self field receivers do not receive field-domain evidence.

Non-tokio Runtime imports, local Runtime types including raw r#Runtime definitions, parent-module-only imports, namespace aliases named tokio, extern crate aliases named tokio, raw identifier aliases such as r#tokio including brace-form aliases, raw local r#tokio module roots, and Runtime imports through those shadowed roots do not prove tokio runtime field identity, including flexible-whitespace alias spellings.
Imported Runtime, nested-brace Runtime, and fully qualified Handle self-field receivers report future-drive and future-settled obligations.
Parent-module imports do not prove child-module field types.

Namespace and extern crate aliases named tokio stay closed, including flexible-whitespace alias spellings, raw identifier aliases such as r#tokio, brace-form raw aliases, raw local r#tokio module roots, and Runtime imports through those shadowed roots.

current_crates_gate.note: The local crates surface has no proof-backed Rust self-field block_on bridge rows.

summary.closed_surface: local function-scope struct fields and crate-local Runtime remain closed in this slice.

closed_surfaces.surface: runner.rt.block_on(...) or other non-self field receivers
closed_surfaces.reason: The receiver identity is not tied to the current impl self receiver.

closed_surfaces.surface: function-local struct Runner { rt: Runtime } with local impl Runner
closed_surfaces.reason: This slice only searches module-scope struct declarations that are siblings of the impl scope.

closed_surfaces.surface: type Rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime; struct Runner { rt: Rt }
closed_surfaces.reason: Rust type aliases are not converted into nominal receiver domain evidence in this slice.

closed_surfaces.surface: fields assigned from runtime constructors rather than declared with a tokio runtime type
closed_surfaces.reason: Constructor assignment and field lifecycle proof are separate capabilities.

closed_surfaces.surface: Runtime fields from project-local tokio roots, raw local r#tokio roots, namespace aliases, extern crate aliases, or non-tokio modules
closed_surfaces.reason: Same-named local or third-party type and namespace roots do not prove tokio runtime receiver semantics.

closed_surfaces.surface: Exact convergence between block_on, await, and synchronous payload values
closed_surfaces.reason: Future drive scheduling, wake/poll behavior, cancellation/liveness, result-channel, and complete receiver/type identity contracts remain missing evidence.

next_followups: Add function-local struct/impl field provenance only after sibling block scope, shadowing, and duplicate-local-type hard negatives are specified.
Consider type-alias to nominal-domain evidence as a separate producer slice.
Consider constructor-assigned runtime fields only after field lifecycle and mutation proof exist.
